title: Crispy Portuguese Cod Stew
description: A one‑pot Portuguese cod stew with a light rum‑infused tomato broth, finished with a golden, crispy cod topping—low‑carb, rich, and satisfying.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large, heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat; add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and diced red bell pepper, cooking for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in the cherry tomatoes and cook until they start to soften, about 2 minutes.
Deglaze the pot with the light rum, allowing it to reduce by half, about 2–3 minutes.
Stir in the fish stock, smoked paprika, salt, pepper, and chili flakes; bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let the broth simmer for 5 minutes to meld the flavors.

While the broth simmers, heat the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil in a non‑stick skillet over high heat. Pat the cod fillets dry, season with salt and pepper, and sear for 1–2 minutes per side until a golden crust forms.
Gently slide the seared cod fillets into the simmering stew, cover, and cook for an additional 3–4 minutes until the fish is opaque but still tender.
Remove the pot from the heat, garnish the stew with chopped cilantro, and serve immediately with crispy cod on top.
